steps.title: Identify what "emphasize a similarity" requires
steps.content: The task asks for a sentence that highlights what the two positions have in common. The notes describe first position (Vaganova method of ballet) and demi-seconde position (Royal Academy of Dance method of ballet) — both are arm positions. A sentence emphasizing a similarity must name both positions and state what they share, not contrast them or describe only one.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "First position in the Vaganova method of ballet and demi-seconde position in the Royal Academy of Dance method of ballet are both positions for the dancer's arms.": Names both positions and states exactly what they share — they are both arm positions. ✓
  • "Both first position and demi-seconde position are positions in the Royal Academy of Dance method of ballet.": Incorrectly attributes first position to the Royal Academy of Dance; first position belongs to the Vaganova method of ballet. ✗
  • "In first position, both arms are rounded to roughly align the fingers with the navel, in contrast to demi-seconde position, where both arms are out to the sides but angled downward.": Explicitly contrasts the two positions rather than emphasizing a similarity. ✗
  • "In the Vaganova method of ballet, there are a number of positions, including first position.": Describes only the Vaganova method of ballet and never mentions demi-seconde position at all, so no comparison is made. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
